dependencies: Limited number of suppliers for materials and services essential to operations including raw materials
Dependence on limited source or sole source suppliers for certain raw materials such as titanium sponge and specialized metal alloys
Supply constraints from key suppliers due to lack of capacity, labor shortages and/or material availability
Approval, license and qualification requirements applicable to manufacturers and suppliers limit alternative sources
risks: Supply chain disruptions could have material adverse effect on business
Several suppliers have had constraints on ability to supply full requirements due to lack of capacity, labor shortages and/or material availability
Supply disruptions due to trade barriers, business continuity, quality, cyberattacks, transportation, delivery or logistics challenges, weather, natural disaster, war, or pandemic events
Geopolitical tensions and conflicts impact global energy markets and raw material costs
Russia-Ukraine conflict may cause global titanium prices to fluctuate or increase
Customers' failure to return titanium revert can result in increased titanium purchases at inflated costs

regulatory_risks: Compliance with government contracting regulations including subcontracting, cybersecurity, and specialty metals requirements

Failure to comply with procurement laws and regulations could result in civil and criminal penalties, termination of contracts, forfeiture of profits, suspension of payments, or suspension from future government contracting
Compliance with U.S. and foreign laws including antitrust and competition regulations, Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and other anti-bribery and corruption laws

Compliance with trade laws including International Traffic in Arms Regulations, Export Administration Regulations, and sanctions/embargoes administered by U.S. Department of Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control
Compliance with health, safety, and environmental laws and regulations including OSHA and foreign government entities
New or revised environmental laws and regulations including cap-and-trade systems and limits on greenhouse gas emissions
Customer decarbonization requirements and carbon pricing or product energy efficiency requirements
Climate-related/sustainability and ESG rules and regulations
OECD Pillar 2 framework introducing 15% global minimum tax implemented through domestic legislation in multiple countries
Export restrictions, sanctions, new or increased import duties or tariffs, and countervailing or anti-dumping duties
Changes in tax laws, regulations, or their interpretation including potential retroactive effect
Governmental constraints on operations in foreign jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Compliance with sanctions, regulations and embargoes administered by U.S. Department of Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control
Russia-Ukraine conflict impacts including sanctions and global titanium supply disruptions
Wars in Ukraine and Middle East creating geopolitical risks
Exposure to fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates due to geopolitical conditions
Global trade landscape growing more volatile including recent executive orders for new tariffs and likelihood of further tariffs and retaliatory counter measures
